binaries.title: fastfat.sys (Windows FAT Filesystem Driver) - EFS Size-Arithmetic Hardening

changed_functions.name: EfspConstructNewPfileHeader (sub_1c0009be8) -> sub_14000a3a4
key_changes: UNPATCHED: Size computation (rax_10 = rdx_3 + (s - a); alloc_size = rax_10 + 0x20) validated with a sequence of inline sign-bit comparisons plus a final alloc_size > 0xffffffff range check and an alloc_size >= 0x20 gate

PATCHED: The additions are performed through the checked-add helper sub_14000a810 (three call sites: rdx_3 + rcx_4, result + 0x20, and DataOffset + rcx_4), which returns STATUS_INTEGER_OVERFLOW on any sign-inconsistent sum; the > 0xffffffff range check and >= 0x20 gate are retained
PATCHED: Pool allocation migrated from ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, size, 0x6d736645) to ExAllocatePool2(POOL_FLAG_PAGED, size, 0x6d736645)
PATCHED: Sensitive key-buffer zeroing changed from memset to a manual loop
changed_functions.security_notes: CWE-190 integer-overflow hardening of a PagedPool size computation. The size inputs DataOffset (*(r12+r13+0x32)), DataLength (*(r12+r13+0x36)), the EFS stream size s, and the base offset a (var_90+8) are all read as 32-bit fields, so rax_10 = rdx_3 + (s - a) stays within roughly +/- 2^33 and cannot approach a 64-bit wrap. In the unpatched build the inline sign-bit checks together with the alloc_size > 0xffffffff and alloc_size >= 0x20 gates already bound the allocation to [0x20, 0xffffffff], which matches the amount written into the 'Efsm' block (0x20 bytes plus a 16-byte UUID). The descriptor and stream data are copied via memmove (sub_1c000e600) into a separate PFile-stream buffer returned by EfspAquirePFileStream, not into the 'Efsm' allocation. The patch reaches the same allocation size through the checked-add helper sub_14000a810 and moves to the modern ExAllocatePool2 API; both builds produce the same size for every input, so the change is defense-in-depth rather than a fix for a reachable under-allocation.

changed_functions.name: DplWrapProtect (sub_1c0008008) (DPL wrap encryption - AES-CBC/HMAC-SHA256)
key_changes: Replaces inline field truncation (0xffff clamps for arg7 struct fields) with helper functions sub_140009954 and sub_14000992c that perform overflow-checked size computations
Changes __builtin_memset zeroing of 32-byte key buffers to manual loop-based zeroing
Struct offsets shifted by +0x10 consistently (compiler/version difference)
changed_functions.security_notes: Version-level API migration and compiler differences. No security-relevant logic change; the encryption routine's overflow checks were already present inline (sbb patterns) in the unpatched build.

changed_functions.name: FatSetupAllocationSupport (sub_1c0025258)
key_changes: VCB struct offsets shifted by +0x10 throughout (layout change between versions)
Reorganizes overflow/bounds checks: the patched build folds a rcx < rdx_2 bound into the initial overflow check; the unpatched build checks it at a later label
Both builds check the same conditions in different code organization
changed_functions.security_notes: Structural reorganization with equivalent validation. The patched build folds a bounds check into the initial overflow check while the unpatched build checks it at a later label. Logically equivalent for all inputs.

changed_functions.name: FatDefragDirectory (sub_1c0038518)
key_changes: Struct offset shifts (+0x10 for VCB fields: 0x130->0x140, 0xc0->0x190, 0x98 list head comparison changed)
Resource list traversal changed from do-while to for-loop (compiler difference)
ExAllocatePoolWithTag used in both builds (no API migration here)
changed_functions.security_notes: Struct layout and compiler differences. The directory defragmentation logic is identical.

changed_functions.name: FatCommonWrite (sub_1c004f438)
key_changes: Call target addresses updated for helper functions
Logic flow is equivalent between both builds
changed_functions.security_notes: Common write path with offset shifts and call-target updates. No security-relevant logic change.

security_findings.entry_point: EFS post-create data path on an EFS-enabled FAT/FAT32 volume
call_chain: File open on an EFS-tagged file (FatEnableEfs set)
EfspConstructNewPfileHeader (sub_1c0009be8) - builds the EFS PFile header and computes the pool size
memmove (sub_1c000e600) - copies descriptor/stream data into the separate EfspAquirePFileStream buffer
security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-190 integer-overflow hardening of a pool-size computation (defense-in-depth)
security_findings.description: EfspConstructNewPfileHeader (sub_1c0009be8) builds an EFS PFile header for a file on a FAT volume. It reads two 32-bit descriptor fields, DataOffset (*(r12+r13+0x32)) and DataLength (*(r12+r13+0x36)), forms rdx_3 = DataOffset + DataLength (with a 32-bit carry check), computes rcx_4 = s - a from two other 32-bit locals (s = EFS stream size, a = var_90+8 base offset), and forms rax_10 = rdx_3 + rcx_4. The allocation size is rax_10 + 0x20. Because all inputs are 32-bit-derived, rax_10 cannot approach a 64-bit wrap. The unpatched build validates the additions with inline sign-bit comparisons plus alloc_size > 0xffffffff and alloc_size >= 0x20 gates, which already bound the allocation to [0x20, 0xffffffff]. The 'Efsm' block receives only 0x20 bytes plus a 16-byte UUID; the larger memmove operations write into a separate PFile-stream buffer returned by EfspAquirePFileStream. The patched build performs the same additions through the checked-add helper sub_14000a810 and allocates via ExAllocatePool2(POOL_FLAG_PAGED). Both builds compute the same allocation size for every input, so this is a hardening/refactor change, not a reachable memory-corruption fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. EFS on FAT enabled (HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Fastfat\FatEnableEfs = 1). 2. A FAT/FAT32 volume with an EFS-tagged file. 3. Opening the file drives EfspPostCreateImpersonateAndGetData, which calls EfspConstructNewPfileHeader and runs the size computation. Both builds validate DataOffset+DataLength carry, rdx_3 <= caller size, alloc_size <= 0xffffffff, and alloc_size >= 0x20 before allocating.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrated. The allocation size is bounded to [0x20, 0xffffffff] in both builds and matches the 0x20 bytes plus UUID written into the 'Efsm' block; the descriptor/stream copies target a separate buffer. The change is a defense-in-depth migration to a checked-add helper and ExAllocatePool2.

breakpoints.why: Entry to the EFS PFile-header builder. arg1 (rcx) is the stream/context pointer; the function reads the EFS descriptor fields and computes the pool size.
Inspect the size: ecx = 1 (PagedPool), edx = alloc_size, r8d = 0x6d736645 ('Efsm'). alloc_size is bounded to [0x20, 0xffffffff] by the preceding checks.

watch: rax_10 = rdx_3 + (s - a) after 0x1C0009D11 - stays within roughly +/- 2^33 (32-bit inputs)
rdx_3 = *(r12+r13+0x36) + *(r12+r13+0x32) - DataLength + DataOffset (32-bit, carry checked at 0x1C0009CDB)
r14 = rax_10 + 0x20 at 0x1C0009D36 - alloc_size; rejected at 0x1C0009D56 if > 0xffffffff
Pool tag 0x6d736645 ('Efsm') and PoolType ecx=1 at the 0x1C0009D78 call
memmove (sub_1c000e600) length at 0x1C0009DD0 targets the PFile-stream buffer rsi, not the 'Efsm' block rdi
key_offsets: 0x1C0009D06: rcx_4 = s - a; rax_10 = rdx_3 + rcx_4
0x1C0009D53: cmp r14, 0xffffffff ; ja overflow (32-bit range gate)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Enable EFS on FAT: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Fastfat\FatEnableEfs = 1. 2. Create a FAT32 volume (VHD or USB) with an EFS-tagged file. 3. Open the file to drive EfspPostCreateImpersonateAndGetData -> EfspConstructNewPfileHeader and the size computation. Both builds reject descriptor fields that would push the size past the 32-bit range or below 0x20.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the 0x1C0009D78 call the size in edx is a validated value in [0x20, 0xffffffff] with tag 'Efsm'. The 'Efsm' block receives 0x20 bytes plus a UUID; the memmove operations copy descriptor/stream data into the separate EfspAquirePFileStream buffer. The patched build reaches the same size through sub_14000a810 and allocates via ExAllocatePool2.
debugging_aids.notes: The unpatched image uses base 0x1c0000000; the patched image uses base 0x140000000 with +0x10 struct offset drift. EFS descriptor fields are at *(r12+r13+0x32) (DataOffset) and *(r12+r13+0x36) (DataLength), where r12 is the EFS context (Src) and r13 = *(r12+0x0e). Copy helper sub_1c000e600 is memmove. The checked-add helper sub_14000a810 in the patched build returns STATUS_INTEGER_OVERFLOW on any sign-inconsistent sum.

summary: The patch hardens the pool-size arithmetic in the EFS PFile-header builder EfspConstructNewPfileHeader (sub_1c0009be8) of fastfat.sys, whose patched counterpart is sub_14000a3a4. The unpatched build validates the size computation (rax_10 = rdx_3 + (s - a); alloc_size = rax_10 + 0x20) with inline sign-bit comparisons plus alloc_size > 0xffffffff and alloc_size >= 0x20 gates. The patched build performs the same additions through the checked-add helper sub_14000a810 (returns STATUS_INTEGER_OVERFLOW on wrap) and migrates ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool) to ExAllocatePool2(POOL_FLAG_PAGED). All size inputs are 32-bit-derived, so the sum cannot approach a 64-bit wrap, and both builds bound the allocation to [0x20, 0xffffffff] - matching the 0x20 bytes plus UUID written into the 'Efsm' block, with descriptor/stream data copied via memmove into a separate PFile-stream buffer. The change is defense-in-depth (CWE-190 hardening), not a fix for a reachable under-allocation. The function is reached from EfspPostCreateImpersonateAndGetData (0x1C0056738), the EFS post-create data path.
